text.div:
Narratif généré : DéfinitionStructure fr-cda-resultat-examens-de-biologie-element-clinique-pertinent

NomDrapeauxCard.TypeDescription et contraintes
Observation ObservationEspace de noms (namespace) XML: urn:hl7-org:v3
Les instances de ce type sont validées par templateId
Conteneur logique: ClinicalDocument (CDA Class)
Slices pour templateId 2..2IISlice: Non ordonné, Ouvert par value:root
templateId:iheLaboratoryObservation 1..1IIConformité Laboratory Observation (IHE PaLM)
@root 1..1oid, uuid, ruidMotif requis: 1.3.6.1.4.1.19376.1.3.1.6
templateId:frResultatExamensDeBiologieElementCliniquePertinent 1..1IIConformité FR-Resultat-examens-de-biologie-element-clinique-pertinent (CI-SIS)
@root 1..1oid, uuid, ruidMotif requis: 1.2.250.1.213.1.1.3.80
@classCode S1..1csMotif requis: OBS
@moodCode S1..1csMotif requis: EVN
id S0..1IIIdentifiant de l'entrée
code S1..1CDCode d'identification de l'analyse ou de l'observation
@code S1..1cs
@codeSystem S1..1oid, uuid, ruidMotif requis: 2.16.840.1.113883.6.1
@displayName S1..1st
originalText S1..1ED
reference S1..1TELRéférence à l'expression verbale dans la partie visualisable du compte-rendu
translation S0..1CDCode d'identification d'attente national ou code de portée locale
statusCode S1..1CSNiveau de complétude. - 'completed' si le résultat est présent ; - 'aborted' dans le cas où l'élément d'examen n’a pu être et ne sera pas réalisé ;
Binding: ActStatus (required)
effectiveTime S0..1IVL_TSDate et heure de ce résultat
value S0..1ANY, BL, ED, ST, CD, CV, CE, CO, SC, II, TEL, AD, EN, INT, REAL, PQ, MO, TS, IVL_PQ, IVL_TS, PIVL_TS, EIVL_TS, SXPR_TS, RTO_PQ_PQValeur du résultat. Le type de valeur (attribut xsi:type) est choisi dynamiquement. Les autres attributs qui qualifient cette valeur (comme par exemple l'unité) dépendent du type de donnée choisi. Les résultats qualitatifs codés peuvent le cas échéant être exprimés dans plusieurs systèmes de codage simultanément. De même les résultats numériques peuvent être exprimés simultanément dans plusieurs unités.
interpretationCode S0..1CECode d'interprétation
Binding: ObservationInterpretation (required)
methodCode S0..1CEMéthode ou technique employée
Binding: JDV Technique Biologie CISIS (required)
subject S0..1FRCDASujetNonHumain, FRCDAPatientAvecSujetNonHumain
performer S0..*FRCDALaboratoireExecutantLaboratoire sous-traitant. Apparaît à ce niveau si et et seulement si ce résultat a été produit par un laboratoire exécutant distinct du laboratoire exécutant déclaré aux niveaux supérieurs.
author S0..*FRCDAAuthorAuteur. Apparaît à ce niveau si le rendu de ce résultat procède de cet auteur spécifique, différent de celui déclaré aux niveaux supérieurs.
participant S0..*FRCDAParticipantCorpsValideur de ces résultats : typeCode='AUTHEN' [0..1] et templateId '1.3.6.1.4.1.19376.1.3.3.1.5' additionnel obligatoire. - Responsable de cet examen : typeCode='RESP' [0..1] - Dispositif automatique impliqué dans la production des résultats : typeCode='DEV' [0..*]
Slices pour entryRelationship S0..*EntryRelationshipSlice: Non ordonné, Ouvert par value:$this
entryRelationship:frCommentaireER 0..*EntryRelationshipCommentaire d'interprétation des résultats
@typeCode 1..1csMotif requis: SUBJ
act 0..1FRCDACommentaireER
entryRelationship:frPrelevement 0..*EntryRelationshipPrélèvement
@typeCode 1..1csMotif requis: COMP
procedure 0..1FRCDAPrelevement
entryRelationship:frResultatsAnterieurs 0..*EntryRelationshipRésultats antérieurs. Plusieurs résultats antérieurs peuvent être ajoutés. Ils doivent être comparables avec le résultat rendu, c'est-à-dire obtenus suivant la même méthode ou une méthode comparable, et exprimés dans la même unité ou dans le même système de codage.
@typeCode 1..1csMotif requis: REFR
observation 1..1Observation
@classCode 1..1csMotif requis: OBS
@moodCode 1..1csMotif requis: EVN
code S1..1CDMême code que l'observation de niveau supérieur
originalText S0..1ED
reference S1..1TEL
translation S0..1CD
statusCode
@code 0..1csMotif requis: completed
effectiveTime S1..1IVL_TSDate de ce résultat antérieur
value S1..1ANY, BL, ED, ST, CD, CV, CE, CO, SC, II, TEL, AD, EN, INT, REAL, PQ, MO, TS, IVL_PQ, IVL_TS, PIVL_TS, EIVL_TS, SXPR_TS, RTO_PQ_PQValeur de ce résultat antérieur
interpretationCode S0..1CEBinding: ObservationInterpretation (required)
methodCode S0..1CE
referenceRange S0..1InfrastructureRoot
observationRange
@classCode 0..1csMotif requis: OBS
value 1..1BL, ED, ST, CD, CV, CE, CO, SC, II, TEL, AD, EN, INT, REAL, PQ, MO, TS, IVL_PQ, IVL_TS, PIVL_TS, EIVL_TS, SXPR_TS
interpretationCode S1..1CE
@code 0..1csMotif requis: N
@codeSystem 0..1oid, uuid, ruidMotif requis: 2.16.840.1.113883.5.83

Documentation pour ce format

name: FRCDAResultatExamensDeBiologieElementCliniquePertinent
title: CDA - FR Resultat examens de biologie element clinique pertinent
publisher: Agence du Numérique en Santé (ANS) - 2-10 Rue d'Oradour-sur-Glane, 75015 Paris

contact.name: Agence du Numérique en Santé (ANS) - 2-10 Rue d'Oradour-sur-Glane, 75015 Paris

description:
Entrée FR-Resultat-examens-de-biologie-element-clinique-pertinent: IHE-PALM - Laboratory Observation. 
 - L'entrée Résultat d'examen / élément clinique pertinent est une entrée de type 'observation' qui permet de décrire un résultat d'un élément d'un examen de biologie médicale ou un élément clinique pertinent fourni par le prescripteur ou le préleveur dans le contexte clinique de la demande d'examens biologiques.

element.short: Base for all types and resources
element.definition: Defines the basic properties of every data value. This is an abstract type, meaning that no value can be just a data value without belonging to any concrete type. Every concrete type is a specialization of this general abstract DataValue type.

element.label: Exceptional Value Detail
element.definition: If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.
When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of realm-specific constraints. The value of this attribute identifies the realm in question

When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.

constraint.human: An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.

element.label: Exceptional Value Detail
element.definition: If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.

element.label: Assigning Authority Name
element.definition: A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.

element.label: Displayable
element.definition: Specifies if the identifier is intended for human display and data entry (displayable = true) as opposed to pure machine interoperation (displayable = false).

element.label: Root
element.definition: Identifies the type as an HL7 Registered model

element.label: Extension
element.definition: A character string as a unique identifier within the scope of the identifier root.
When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of a set of template-defined constraints. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the templates in question

element.label: Code
element.definition: The plain code symbol defined by the code system. For example, "784.0" is the code symbol of the ICD-9 code "784.0" for headache.

element.label: Code System
element.definition: Specifies the code system that defines the code.

element.label: Code System Name
element.definition: The common name of the coding system.

element.label: Code System Version
element.definition: If applicable, a version descriptor defined specifically for the given code system.

element.label: Display Name
element.definition: A name or title for the code, under which the sending system shows the code value to its users.
The valueSet extension adds an attribute for elements with a CD dataType which indicates the particular value set constraining the coded concept.
The valueSetVersion extension adds an attribute for elements with a CD dataType which indicates the version of the particular value set constraining the coded concept.

element.label: Original Text
element.definition: The text or phrase used as the basis for the coding.

element.label: Exceptional Value Detail
element.definition: If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.

element.label: Compression
element.definition: Indicates whether the raw byte data is compressed, and what compression algorithm was used.

element.label: Integrity Check
element.definition: The integrity check is a short binary value representing a cryptographically strong checksum that is calculated over the binary data. The purpose of this property, when communicated with a reference is for anyone to validate later whether the reference still resolved to the same data that the reference resolved to when the encapsulated data value with reference was created.

element.label: Integrity Check Algorithm
element.definition: Specifies the algorithm used to compute the integrityCheck value. The cryptographically strong checksum algorithm Secure Hash Algorithm-1 (SHA-1) is currently the industry standard. It has superseded the MD5 algorithm only a couple of years ago, when certain flaws in the security of MD5 were discovered. Currently the SHA-1 hash algorithm is the default choice for the integrity check algorithm. Note that SHA-256 is also entering widespread usage.

element.label: Language
element.definition: For character based information the language property specifies the human language of the text.

element.label: Media Type
element.definition: Identifies the type of the encapsulated data and identifies a method to interpret or render the data.

element.short: Allows for mixed text content. If @representation='B64', this SHALL be a base64binary string.
element.definition: Data that is primarily intended for human interpretation or for further machine processing is outside the scope of HL7. This includes unformatted or formatted written language, multimedia data, or structured information as defined by a different standard (e.g., XML-signatures.)
element.comment: This element is represented in XML as textual content. The actual name "xmlText" will not appear in a CDA instance.

element.short: Référence à l'expression verbale dans la partie visualisable du compte-rendu

element.label: Thumbnail
element.definition: An abbreviated rendition of the full data. A thumbnail requires significantly fewer resources than the full data, while still maintaining some distinctive similarity with the full data. A thumbnail is typically used with by-reference encapsulated data. It allows a user to select data more efficiently before actually downloading through the reference.

element.definition: Specifies additional codes that increase the specificity of the the primary code.

When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of a set of template-defined constraints. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the templates in question
The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to "true" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.

The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at "true", whereas here the value is defaulted to "true", and can be changed to "false" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.

Relates an Observation to the ObservationRange class, where the expected range of values for a particular observation can be specified.

element.label: Exceptional Value Detail
element.definition: If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.
When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of realm-specific constraints. The value of this attribute identifies the realm in question

When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.

constraint.human: An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.

element.label: Exceptional Value Detail
element.definition: If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.

element.label: Assigning Authority Name
element.definition: A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.

element.label: Displayable
element.definition: Specifies if the identifier is intended for human display and data entry (displayable = true) as opposed to pure machine interoperation (displayable = false).

element.label: Root
element.definition: Identifies the type as an HL7 Registered model

element.label: Extension
element.definition: A character string as a unique identifier within the scope of the identifier root.

element.label: Code
element.definition: The plain code symbol defined by the code system. For example, "784.0" is the code symbol of the ICD-9 code "784.0" for headache.

element.label: Code System
element.definition: Specifies the code system that defines the code.

element.label: Code System Name
element.definition: The common name of the coding system.

element.label: Code System Version
element.definition: If applicable, a version descriptor defined specifically for the given code system.

element.label: Display Name
element.definition: A name or title for the code, under which the sending system shows the code value to its users.
The valueSet extension adds an attribute for elements with a CD dataType which indicates the particular value set constraining the coded concept.
The valueSetVersion extension adds an attribute for elements with a CD dataType which indicates the version of the particular value set constraining the coded concept.

element.label: Original Text
element.definition: The text or phrase used as the basis for the coding.

element.label: Qualifier
element.definition: Specifies additional codes that increase the specificity of the the primary code.

element.label: Translation
element.definition: A set of other concept descriptors that translate this concept descriptor into other code systems.

The sdtc:precondition1 extension allows for the association of a criterion with a reference range (ObservationRange), which allows the expression in a lab report that a reference range is conditional on some criterion such as patient sex or age (or a combination of criterion).

When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of a set of template-defined constraints. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the templates in question

A code specifying the logical conjunction of the criteria among all the condition-links of Acts (e.g., and, or, exclusive-or).

All AND criteria must be true.

If OR and AND criteria occur together, one criterion out of the OR-group must be true and all AND criteria must be true also.

If XOR criteria occur together with OR and AND criteria, exactly one of the XOR criteria must be true, and at least one of the OR criteria and all AND criteria must be true.

In other words, the sets of AND, OR, and XOR criteria are in turn combined by a logical AND operator (all AND criteria and at least one OR criterion and exactly one XOR criterion).

Niveau de complétude

element.short: Date et heure de ce résultat
element.definition: Date et heure de ce résultat

element.short: Valeur du résultat. Le type de valeur (attribut xsi:type) est choisi dynamiquement. Les autres attributs qui qualifient cette valeur (comme par exemple l'unité) dépendent du type de donnée choisi. Les résultats qualitatifs codés peuvent le cas échéant être exprimés dans plusieurs systèmes de codage simultanément. De même les résultats numériques peuvent être exprimés simultanément dans plusieurs unités.
element.definition: Valeur du résultat

element.short: Code d'interprétation
element.definition: Code d'interprétation

element.short: Méthode ou technique employée
element.definition: Méthode ou technique employée

element.short: Laboratoire sous-traitant. Apparaît à ce niveau si et et seulement si ce résultat a été produit par un laboratoire exécutant distinct du laboratoire exécutant déclaré aux niveaux supérieurs.
element.definition: Laboratoire sous-traitant

element.short: Auteur. Apparaît à ce niveau si le rendu de ce  résultat procède de cet auteur spécifique, différent de celui déclaré aux niveaux supérieurs.
element.definition: Auteur

element.short:
Valideur de ces résultats : typeCode='AUTHEN' [0..1] et templateId '1.3.6.1.4.1.19376.1.3.3.1.5' additionnel obligatoire. 
- Responsable de cet examen : typeCode='RESP' [0..1]
- Dispositif automatique impliqué dans la production des résultats : typeCode='DEV' [0..*]
Commentaire d'interprétation des résultats
element.definition: Commentaire d'interprétation des résultats

element.short: Prélèvement
element.definition: Prélèvement

element.short: Résultats antérieurs. Plusieurs résultats antérieurs peuvent être ajoutés. Ils doivent être comparables avec le résultat rendu, c'est-à-dire obtenus suivant la même méthode ou une méthode comparable, et exprimés dans la même unité ou dans le même système de codage.
element.definition: Résultats antérieurs

element.short: Même code que l'observation de niveau supérieur
